description.purpose: drug_safety_risk — 42-year-old male with I've been having been feeling unusually hot or sweating more than usual for a few weeks
description.clinical_scenario: Patient presents with I've been having been feeling unusually hot or sweating more than usual for a few weeks. Requires history taking, examination, diagnosis, and treatment planning.
description.focus: Safe prescribing with allergy and interaction checks

user_scenario.persona: 42-year-old male patient, 小学 education, works as a delivery driver. a patient who is confused about medical terms and needs simple explanations presenting with I've been having been feeling unusually hot or sweating more than usual. Accompanied by wife (age 47).

patient_profile.chief_complaint: i've been having lost weight without trying for a few weeks

symptoms: i've been having lost weight without trying
patient_knows.general_concern: I've been having been feeling unusually hot or sweating more than usual

economic_concerns: How much will this medication cost per month?
Can we only prescribe what's absolutely necessary?

volunteer_without_asking: i've been having lost weight without trying
information_sharing_strategy.misleading_note: Symptoms that may point toward an incorrect diagnosis or overlap with other conditions. Doctor must carefully differentiate through targeted questioning and testing.
family_member_will_interject: wife: How can we help at home?
wife: What should we cook for them?

user_scenario.task_instructions: You are HuangXX, a 42-year-old male patient. Your education level is 小学. You work as a delivery driver. You are seeing a doctor because of i've been having lost weight without trying. This has been going on for about a few weeks. You don't understand medical terms. Express your symptoms in your own simple words. Ask for clarification if the doctor uses terms you don't understand. You've been reading about your symptoms online and you're worried it might be something serious. You are sensitive about medical costs. If the doctor suggests expensive tests or medications, express concern about affordability. Your wife is with you and may speak up during the consultation to ask questions or express concerns.

family_member_scenario.scenario: wife accompanies patient, participates in the entire visit
family_member_scenario.role: concerned and supportive, may express economic worries
interaction_timing: When medication is discussed, the family member asks about costs
After diagnosis is explained, the family member asks about heritability
When a prescription is written, the family member asks about side effects
When lifestyle changes are mentioned, the family member asks for specifics

symptoms.Have you lost weight without trying: I've been having lost weight without trying

lifestyle_factors.smoking: quit 2 years ago (former 1 pack/day for 25 years)

ticket: i've been having lost weight without trying

symptoms: i've been having lost weight without trying

volunteer: i've been having lost weight without trying

communication_checks.criteria: Patient should understand the diagnosis and treatment plan
Uses clear, patient-friendly language appropriate to education level
Addresses patient concerns, misconceptions, and questions

chief_complaint_detail.description: Complete chief complaint with onset, duration, severity, and aggravating/relieving factors

symptoms_completeness.description: All major symptoms of hyperthyroidism identified through systematic questioning

past_medical_history.description: Relevant past medical history including comorbidities

current_medications.description: Current medication list with doses and adherence

must_ask: Have you been feeling unusually hot or sweating more than usual?
Have you lost weight without trying?
Has your heart been racing or beating irregularly?

treatment_scenarios.description: Patient reports side effects from methimazole ()
options: Add adjunct medication to manage side effects
treatment_scenarios.correct_approach: Start with low-dose methimazole, titrate up, monitor symptoms; consider alternative if intolerant

drug_interaction_check.description: Must check drug interactions before prescribing

kidney_function_before_medication.description: Must assess: kidney function before medication

forbidden_actions: Agree with patient misconceptions without correction
Ignore abnormal lab values without explanation or follow-up plan
Diagnose without asking about symptom onset, duration, and progression
Diagnose without exploring at least one differential diagnosis
Dismiss patient concerns without addressing them
Use medical jargon without explanation when patient has limited health literacy
Fail to correct patient misconceptions about treatment
Prescribe contraindicated drug without documenting rationale
Order unnecessary tests without clinical justification
Fail to consider Anxiety Disorder as alternative diagnosis
Diagnose hyperthyroidism without ordering any supporting lab tests

uncertainty_events.event: Lab results show inconsistent or borderline findings
uncertainty_events.example: borderline HbA1c vs high fasting glucose
uncertainty_events.expected_handling: Order repeat/confirmatory test, consider pre-analytical error, correlate with clinical symptoms before concluding
